mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) signals a strengthening demand for housing, which in turn boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening-demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 110 signals a weakening supply chain, which in turn reduces production costs and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening-supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) above 4.7 signals a weakening labor market, which in turn reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in weakening-labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real personal income (RSAFS) above $370,000 signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which in turn boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening-demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 110 signals a weakening supply chain, which in turn reduces production costs and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening-supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) above 4.7 signals a weakening labor market, which in turn reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in weakening-labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real personal income (RSAFS) above $370,000 and a 1-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) above 1642 signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which in turn boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening-demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 110 and a 2-month decline in personal income (RSAFS) below $370,000 signals a weakening supply chain and a reduction in consumer spending, which in turn drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in weakening-supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
